source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device

event.incident_type: Crush injury from falling storage cabinet
actions_taken: Contract employee was brought to medic
Contract employee was flown in for additional treatment
BSEE Lafayette District conducted onsite investigation on July 6, 2015
event.summary: On June 29, 2015 at approximately 1200 hours, a contract employee was injured while attempting to reposition a storage cabinet to be blasted and painted. Two contract employees were moving the cabinet when they attempted to lay it down. The cabinet door flew open and came back down, making contact with the contract employee's left thumb, resulting in a fractured thumb and nearly detached thumbnail. Contributing factors included failure to complete a Job Safety Analysis (JSA), language barrier (employee understood very little English with no translator available), and lack of communication between employees about the task.

hazards.name: Pinch point hazard from cabinet door and frame
Communication failure due to language barrier

threats.name: Cabinet dropped without proper warning or communication
Employee hand/fingers positioned in pinch point during cabinet repositioning
Cabinet door opened and closed on employee's hand

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and safe work procedures prior to task execution

supporting_text: Prior to attempting to move the cabinet to be blasted and painted the contract employees failed to complete a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for the task that may have reviewed hazards such as pinch points and safer ways to complete the project.
The employees failed to complete a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) prior to the task that could have possibly identified the hazards involved.

controls.name: Pre-task communication and briefing
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all workers understand the task and hazards before work begins

supporting_text: Two employees were attempting to lay the storage cabinet down but did not discuss how they would do so prior to the task.
The CE was told by the other employee that he was laying the cabinet down but the CE understood very little English and may not have known the cabinet was being dropped.

controls.name: Translator availability for non-English speaking workers
controls.barrier_role: Ensure language barrier does not prevent understanding of safety-critical instructions

supporting_text: There was no translator on the facility.

the injured employee could not speak very good English and it is unknown if the injured employee understood the job task prior to repositioning the cabinet. There were no translators on board at the time of the incident.

controls.name: Hand/finger positioning awareness and pinch point avoidance
controls.barrier_role: Prevent worker hands/fingers from being positioned in pinch points during equipment movement

supporting_text: As the employee pushed the cabinet, the CE held on to the edge of the cabinet allowing his fingers to be in a pinch point situation.
CE held on to the edge of the cabinet allowing his fingers to be in a pinch point situation.

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (Hyflex gloves)
controls.barrier_role: Provide hand protection against crush injuries

supporting_text: The CE was wearing Hyflex gloves at the time of the incident.
When the CE removed his glove he discovered his thumb nail had been nearly detached

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
